info.title: Graft Server API
info.description:
Establishes the OpenAPI-owned contract and validation loop for the existing
server runtime while preserving current business behavior, response envelope
semantics, and auth/session transport ownership.

get.description:
Returns the plain runtime health payload registered in `server/internal/app/runtime.go`.
This endpoint is intentionally not wrapped in the standard API envelope.

post.description: Authenticates the caller, writes the refresh cookie, and returns the existing success envelope.

Set-Cookie.description:
Writes the HTTP-only refresh cookie. Cookie name, path, secure flag, and SameSite
behavior follow the current server auth config.

post.summary: Rotate refresh session and issue a new access token
post.description:
Reads the configured refresh cookie, validates the current refresh session, rotates it,
and returns the existing success envelope.

Set-Cookie.description: Writes the rotated HTTP-only refresh cookie.

post.description: Revokes the current refresh session referenced by the refresh cookie and clears that cookie.

get.description: Returns the authenticated bootstrap payload used by the admin shell after login.
Returns the current authenticated user's refresh-session summaries.

post.description: Revokes all refresh sessions for the current authenticated user and clears the refresh cookie.

Set-Cookie.description: Clears the existing refresh cookie when present.

post.description: Revokes all refresh sessions for the current authenticated user except the current session.
Revokes the specified refresh session belonging to the current authenticated user.

Set-Cookie.description: Clears the refresh cookie when the revoked session matches the current session.

post.description: Changes the current authenticated user's password using the current password plus the new password.

400.description: Invalid request or password validation failure.

value.message: 新密码不符合安全要求
新密码不能与当前密码相同

post.description: Completes the restricted-session required password change flow for the current authenticated user.

400.description: Invalid request or password validation failure.

value.message: 新密码不符合安全要求
新密码不能与当前密码相同

get.description: Returns the user list with minimal embedded role summaries so the frontend does not need per-row role reads.

post.description:
Creates a managed backend user and returns the existing success envelope. The current password policy requires at least
12 characters and both letters and digits.

400.description: Invalid request or password policy violation.

value.message: 新密码不符合安全要求

get.description: Returns the existing managed-user summary for the specified user id.

post.description: Updates the managed user profile fields that the existing backend route already accepts.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Target user id.

post.description: Updates the managed user status with the existing backend enable/disable semantics.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Target user id.

post.description: Resets the managed user password using the existing backend reset semantics.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Target user id.

400.description: Invalid request or password policy violation.

value.message: 新密码不符合安全要求
新密码不能重复使用默认密码或当前密码

post.description: Deletes the specified managed user without changing current backend delete semantics.

400.description: Invalid user id or protected self-delete attempt.

get.description: Lists the target user's refresh-session summaries through the admin user-session route.

400.description: Invalid user id or limit parameter.

post.summary: Revoke one session for one user
post.description: Revokes the specified refresh session for the target user through the admin user-session route.

Set-Cookie.description: Clears the refresh cookie when the revoked session matches the current browser session.

400.description: Invalid user id or session id.

post.summary: Revoke all sessions for one user
post.description: Revokes all refresh sessions for the target user through the admin user-session route.

Set-Cookie.description: Clears the refresh cookie when the target user matches the current browser session user.

post.description:
Creates a role with the existing backend normalization behavior for `name`,
`display`, and optional `description`.

get.summary: List permission bindings for one role
get.description: Returns the permission id snapshot currently bound to the target role.

post.description: Replaces the role's permission bindings with the provided stable permission id set.
Adds the provided stable permission ids to the target role without clearing existing bindings.
Removes the provided stable permission ids from the target role.

Updates a role with the existing backend normalization behavior for `name`,
`display`, and optional `description`.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Target role id.

post.description: Enables or disables one role under the RBAC lifecycle rules.
Soft deletes one custom disabled role when the RBAC lifecycle rules permit deletion.

get.summary: List role bindings for one user
get.description: Returns the role id snapshot currently bound to the target user.

post.summary: Replace role bindings for one user
post.description: Replaces the target user's role bindings with the supplied role id snapshot.

400.description: Invalid user id or invalid role id list.

post.summary: Add role bindings for one user
post.description: Adds the supplied role ids to the target user without clearing existing bindings.

400.description: Invalid user id or invalid role id list.

post.summary: Remove role bindings for one user
post.description: Removes the supplied role ids from the target user.

400.description: Invalid user id or invalid role id list.

post.summary: Replace role bindings for many users
post.description: Replaces the role bindings for the supplied user id set in one batch transaction.

400.description: Invalid user id set or role id set.

post.summary: Add role bindings for many users
post.description: Adds the supplied role ids to the supplied user id set in one batch transaction.

400.description: Invalid user id set or role id set.

post.summary: Remove role bindings for many users
post.description: Removes the supplied role ids from the supplied user id set in one batch transaction.

400.description: Invalid user id set or role id set.

get.description: Returns a guarded paged audit-log list for MVP read-only inspection.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Stable business drilldown scope. When present, scope-owned fields remain read-only until the client exits drilldown or converts to normal filters.

parameters.name: business_category
parameters.description: Backend-owned editable business category used by normal filters and scope conversion.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Repeated priority-ordered sort expression using `field:dir` semantics such as `created_at:desc`.

400.description: Invalid list filter, scope conflict, disabled scope, or pagination query.

get.description: Returns one immutable audit-log evidence record for drawer-based inspection.
Returns real aggregated audit-overview data for the selected window.
Returns the audit-owned incident context for one stable security timeline seed event.

404.description: Audit incident seed event was not found.

get.description: Returns the current aggregated monitor server-status snapshot and optional redis-backed trend points.

modules.name: audit
modules.status_detail: Runtime metadata is present and platform signals are healthy

modules.name: user
modules.status_detail: Runtime metadata is present and platform signals are healthy

modules.name: rbac
modules.status_detail: Runtime metadata is present and platform signals are healthy

modules.name: monitor
modules.status_detail: Runtime metadata is present and platform signals are healthy

anomalies.summary: CPU usage is elevated for the current monitor window.

evidence_links.title: Review audit activity around this CPU spike
evidence_links.reason: Security or administrative activity in the same window may explain the current CPU pressure.

get.description: Returns the read-only compile-time module runtime snapshot for the current process.
Returns one module item from the read-only compile-time module runtime snapshot.

404.description: Module runtime item was not found.

get.description: Returns Scheduled Tasks that bind Job Definitions to cron schedules.

post.description: Creates one Scheduled Task bound to a registered Job Definition.

get.description: Returns registered Job Definitions that can be bound to Scheduled Tasks.
Returns one registered Job Definition that can be bound to Scheduled Tasks.

404.description: Scheduler Job Definition was not found.

get.description: Returns one Scheduled Task schedule definition with its latest run state.

put.description: Updates mutable Scheduled Task fields without changing the bound Job Definition.

400.description: Builtin Scheduled Task cannot be deleted.

post.description: Enables one Scheduled Task and dynamically registers its cron schedule.

400.description: Scheduled task cannot be enabled because its definition is invalid.

post.description: Disables one Scheduled Task and dynamically removes its cron schedule.

400.description: Scheduled task cannot be disabled because of its current lifecycle constraints.

get.description: Returns persisted Job Run history for one Scheduled Task.
Returns one persisted Job Run history record for a Scheduled Task.

404.description: Scheduled task or run was not found.

post.description: Triggers one Scheduled Task immediately and returns the persisted manual Job Run result.

400.description: Scheduled task cannot be run because its current definition is invalid or disabled.

post.description: Executes one backend-defined Scheduled Task action and returns a non-persisted JobRunResult.

400.description: Scheduled task action cannot run because config validation failed.

404.description: Scheduled task or action was not found.

get.description: Returns module-registered ConfigDefinitions merged with user overrides. Sensitive values are masked and never returned as plaintext.
Returns one registered ConfigDefinition merged with its user override. Sensitive values are masked and never returned as plaintext.

put.description: Stores user override JSON only. Sensitive responses remain masked and do not echo plaintext values.

post.description: Deletes the user override and returns the module default as the effective value. Sensitive responses remain masked.

get.description: Returns the canonical paged access-log explorer surface.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Canonical fuzzy match applied to `request_id`, `path`, and `username`.

parameters.name: status_group
parameters.description: Repeated status-code group filter for builder presets.

parameters.name: started_from
parameters.description: Inclusive canonical request-time lower bound on `started_at`.

parameters.name: started_to
parameters.description: Inclusive canonical request-time upper bound on `started_at`.

parameters.name: occurred_from
parameters.description: Optional completed-time lower bound on `occurred_at` for secondary filtering.

parameters.name: occurred_to
parameters.description: Optional completed-time upper bound on `occurred_at` for secondary filtering.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Repeated priority-ordered sort expression using `field:dir` semantics such as `started_at:desc`.

get.description: Returns the canonical paged App Log Explorer surface owned by the logger runtime.

parameters.name: occurred_from
parameters.description: Inclusive canonical occurrence-time lower bound on `occurred_at`.

parameters.name: occurred_to
parameters.description: Inclusive canonical occurrence-time upper bound on `occurred_at`.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Canonical fuzzy match applied to component, operation, message, and error.

parameters.name: message
parameters.description: Bounded fuzzy match applied to the app-log message only.

parameters.name: error
parameters.description: Bounded fuzzy match applied to the app-log error text only.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Repeated sort fields encoded as `field:direction`.

post.description: Explicitly deletes selected retained app-log rows without changing the logger-owned retention cleanup policy.

404.description: One or more app logs were not found.

delete.description: Explicitly deletes one retained app-log row without changing the logger-owned retention cleanup policy.

get.description: Returns the fixed dashboard system summary plus quick links and insight widgets visible to the current user.
Returns one visible dashboard widget by id for focused refresh and retry flows.

404.description: Dashboard widget was not found or is not visible to the current user.

get.description: Returns paged notification deliveries visible to the current user.
Returns the unread notification delivery count for the current user.

post.summary: Mark one current-user notification as read
post.description: Marks one notification delivery visible to the current user as read.

404.description: Notification delivery was not found in the current-user scope.

post.description: Marks all current-user notifications, or a filtered current-user subset, as read.

200.description: Count of notification deliveries marked read.

delete.description: Deletes one notification delivery from the current-user view.

404.description: Notification delivery was not found in the current-user scope.

get.description: Returns a paged management announcement list.

post.description: Creates an announcement draft for later publication.

get.description: Returns one management announcement record.

409.description: Published announcements must be archived before deletion.

value.message: 已发布公告需先归档后删除

post.description: Publishes a draft announcement or re-publishes an archived announcement. The server writes published_at to the current service time and published_by to the actor. publish_at is the visibility start time; omitted or null stores publish_at as null for immediate visibility after publish.
Archives a published announcement and hides it from current-user listings.

get.description: Returns announcements visible to the current authenticated user.

post.summary: Mark one current-user announcement as read
post.description: Marks one visible announcement as read for the current user.

404.description: Announcement was not found in the current-user visible scope.

post.summary: Mark all current-user announcements as read
post.description: Marks all currently visible announcements as read for the current user.

200.description: Count of newly marked read announcements.

get.description: Returns the count of currently visible unread announcements for the current user.
Returns container summaries for the configured container runtime.

post.description: Executes one container action for selected containers and returns per-item success or failure.

get.description: Returns one container detail without exposing sensitive environment variables.
Returns bounded container log lines from stdout and stderr.

400.description: Invalid container reference or log query.

post.description: Starts one stopped container when dangerous actions are enabled.

409.description: Container state does not allow this action.

post.description: Stops one running container when dangerous actions are enabled.

409.description: Container state does not allow this action.

500.description: Internal server error under existing error envelope semantics.

post.description: Restarts one container when dangerous actions are enabled.

409.description: Container state does not allow this action.

post.description: Removes one container when dangerous actions are enabled. Running containers require force=true.

400.description: Invalid container reference or remove request.

409.description: Container state does not allow this action.

message.description: Existing runtime fallback text. Consumers should not treat this as the canonical localization contract when a key field is present.

messageKey.description: Stable localization key for key-aware error flows. When present, consumers should treat it as canonical and use message only as fallback text.

locale.description: Present on localized error flows and omitted on normal success.

traceId.description: Mirrors the request id contract used by the current runtime.

message.description: Existing runtime fallback text paired with messageKey for compatibility.

messageKey.description: Stable error localization key. Consumers should prefer this key and use message only as fallback text.

locale.description: Locale used to resolve the fallback message text in this response.

data.description: Optional structured error details preserved from the current runtime.

order.description: Canonical menu order declared by the backend. Lower values render first within the same parent.

title.description: Existing menu-title fallback text. Consumers should prefer title_key when present.

title_key.description: Stable menu title localization key owned by the menu contract. When present, it is the canonical title field and title is fallback-only.

roles.description: Minimal role summaries embedded in the user list to avoid row-level role fetch fanout.

password.description: Initial password. The current server policy requires at least 12 characters and both letters and digits.

name.description: Stable role name. The server trims surrounding whitespace and rejects an empty result.

display.description: User-facing role display name. The server trims surrounding whitespace and rejects an empty result.

description.description: Optional role description. The server trims surrounding whitespace and normalizes empty strings to null.

permission_ids.description: Replaces the role's permission bindings with the provided stable permission id set.

name.description: Stable role name. The server trims surrounding whitespace and rejects an empty result.

display.description: User-facing role display name. The server trims surrounding whitespace and rejects an empty result.

description.description: Optional role description. The server trims surrounding whitespace and normalizes empty strings to null.

display.description: Server-provided fallback display text. Consumers should prefer display_key when present.

display_key.description: Stable localization key for the permission display text.

description.description: Server-provided fallback description text. Consumers should prefer description_key when present.

description_key.description: Stable localization key for the permission description text.

audit-evidence-context.description: Optional audit evidence filters used to correlate evidence links and narrow audit investigation context.

action.description: Exact audit action identifier to match.

action_prefix.description: Action namespace prefix used for broader matching such as `user.`.

resource_type.description: Resource type associated with the audit record.

resource_id.description: Stable resource identifier associated with the audit record.

resource_name.description: Human-readable resource label captured with the audit record.

request_id.description: Correlation request identifier used to trace related records.

result.description: Audit result classification for the matching evidence set.

risk_level.description: Risk classification for matched audit evidence.

created_from.description: Inclusive RFC 3339 lower bound for audit record creation time.

created_to.description: Inclusive RFC 3339 upper bound for audit record creation time.

title.description: Direct evidence-link title fallback when the client has no translation for title_key.

title_key.description: Stable localization key for the evidence-link title.

audit-incident-monitor-evidence.description: Monitor evidence linked to an audit incident, describing anomaly scope, observation time, and deep-link evidence navigation.

state.description: Availability of correlated monitor evidence for this incident.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of the correlated monitor evidence.

reason.description: Optional explanation when evidence is partial or unavailable.

anomaly_key.description: Canonical anomaly identifier describing the monitor condition tied to the incident.

scope_kind.description: Scope category that owns the anomaly target.

scope_ref.description: Stable scope identifier used to reopen the owning monitor view.

observed_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the anomaly was observed.

evidence_links.description: Canonical evidence links that deep-link into related monitor or audit investigation surfaces.

capacity.description: Configured pool capacity used for utilization display.

max_active_connections.description: Configured hard active-connection limit when the client exposes one.

open_connections.description: Current established connections tracked by the pool.

in_use_connections.description: Current connections checked out by requests.

idle_connections.description: Current idle connections available for reuse.

usage_percent.description: Current pool utilization percentage based on in-use connections over display capacity.

wait_count.description: Cumulative number of waits for a pooled connection.

timeout_count.description: Cumulative number of pool wait timeouts.

stale_count.description: Cumulative number of stale or lifetime-expired connections closed by the pool.

example.name: monitor
example.status_detail: Runtime metadata is present and platform signals are healthy

job_key.description: Stable Job Definition key executed by this Scheduled Task.

cron_expression.description: Cron expression validated by the scheduler runtime.

enabled.description: Whether the Scheduled Task is enabled in the DB-backed schedule definition.

builtin.description: Builtin Scheduled Tasks cannot be deleted or change task_key/job_key.

config_json.description: Scheduled Task instance config override JSON object string.

config_source.description: system means using Job Definition default_config; user means applying task config_json override.

effective_config.description: Effective config JSON object string computed by merging Job Definition default_config with task config_json.

next_run_at.description: Reserved for future runtime prediction. Omitted when the current runtime cannot expose a stable next-run value.

job_key.description: Stable Job Definition key this Scheduled Task executes.

cron_expression.description: Cron expression validated by the scheduler runtime.

config_json.description: JSON object string validated by the scheduler runtime before it is passed to the Job Definition handler.

job_key.description: Stable Job Definition key registered by a module.

module_key.description: Module that declares this Job Definition.

title.description: Full Job Definition title fallback when the client has no translation for title_key.

short_title.description: Compact Job Definition title fallback for list display.

description.description: Direct display fallback when the client has no translation for description_key.

config_schema.description: JSON Schema string for Scheduled Task config accepted by this Job Definition.

default_config.description: Default config JSON object string for a new Scheduled Task bound to this Job Definition.

default_cron.description: Default cron expression declared by the Job Definition.

default_enabled.description: Whether new Scheduled Tasks for this Job Definition should be enabled by default.

enabled.description: Whether this Job Definition is active and can be selected.

actions.description: Backend-defined one-shot actions available for this Job Definition.

config_json.description: JSON object string validated by the scheduler runtime before it is passed to the Job Definition handler.

effective_config.description: Effective config JSON object string used by an immediate manual run response when available.

config_json.description: JSON object merged into the action effective config.

effective_config.description: Effective config JSON object string used by the action execution.

key.description: Stable ConfigDefinition key registered by a module.

module.description: Module that owns the ConfigDefinition authority.

domain.description: Configuration domain key used for domain-first settings navigation.

domain_key.description: Stable localization key for the domain display label.

domain_label.description: Direct domain-label fallback when the client has no translation for domain_key.

group.description: Module-declared grouping key for the settings UI.

group_key.description: Stable localization key for the group display label.

group_label.description: Direct group-label fallback when the client has no translation for group_key.

group_description.description: Direct group-description fallback when the client has no translation for group_description_key.

group_description_key.description: Stable localization key for the group description.

title.description: Direct title fallback when the client has no translation for title_key.

title_key.description: Stable localization key for the config item title.

description.description: Direct description fallback when the client has no translation for description_key.

description_key.description: Stable localization key for the config item description.

tags.description: Module-declared technical tags for filtering and display.

config_schema.description: JSON Schema compatible object declared by the module, including Graft x-i18n extensions.

status.description: Whether the effective value is using the module default or a stored user override.

default_value.description: JSON string for the module default; null when sensitive=true.

effective_value.description: JSON string after applying a user override; null when sensitive=true.

override_value.description: User override JSON string; null when no override or sensitive=true.

has_override.description: Whether system_config_values currently stores a user override for this key.

updated_at.description: Last write time for the current user override; null when status=default.

updated_by_user_id.description: User ID that last wrote the current override; null when unavailable or status=default.

updated_by_username.description: Username for updated_by_user_id when it can be resolved.

sensitive.description: Whether plaintext values must not be returned to clients.

masked.description: True when value fields are intentionally withheld from the response.

masked_placeholder.description: Stable display placeholder for masked sensitive values.

restart_required.description: Whether changing this config requires a service restart before it takes effect.

permission.description: Permission code required to update this config item when the definition declares one.

order.description: Module-declared display order within the config group.

value.description: JSON value to store as the user override for the registered definition.

label_key.description: Stable i18n key for the action label. Consumers should prefer this key before label fallback.

label.description: Direct label fallback when the client has no translation for label_key.

notification-navigation-kind.description: Structured navigation target kind. Consumers resolve this with navigation_payload instead of storing URLs.

payload.description: Structured payload owned by the source module and resolved by the frontend notification module.

notification-target-type.description: Delivery target type retained for current-user read-model traceability.

notification-status.description: Current-user notification read-state filter and read model value.

title_key.description: Stable localization key for the notification title when supplied by the source module.

message_key.description: Stable localization key for the notification body when supplied by the source module.

category_key.description: Stable localization key for the notification category display label.

source_key.description: Stable localization key for the notification source display label.

level_key.description: Stable localization key for the notification level display label.

event_type_key.description: Stable localization key for the notification event type display label.

resource_type_key.description: Stable localization key for the notification resource type display label.

action_label_key.description: Stable localization key for the primary business action label.

action_label.description: Fallback business action label when action_label_key is missing or unresolved.

context.description: Source-provided interpolation context for key-first notification rendering.

publish_at.description: Announcement visibility start time. Null means the announcement becomes visible immediately after publish. Future values schedule visibility; past values preserve a backfilled effective time.

published_at.description: Time of the latest publish or re-publish action. Used for audit and management display only; it does not participate in current-user visibility.

published_by.description: User id that performed the latest publish or re-publish action. Existing rows may remain null.

archived_at.description: Manual archive time. Meaningful only when status is archived and cleared on publish or re-publish.

expire_at.description: Announcement visibility end time. Null means long-term valid; expiry hides the announcement from current-user endpoints without changing status.

read_at.description: Current user's read time when returned by current-user endpoints.

unread.description: Current-user unread state when returned by current-user endpoints.

publish_at.description: Announcement visibility start time. Null means immediately visible after publish.

expire_at.description: Announcement visibility end time. Null means long-term valid.

publish_at.description: Announcement visibility start time. Updating this field changes only the effective visibility window, not the latest publish action time.

expire_at.description: Announcement visibility end time. Null means long-term valid.

publish_at.description: Optional visibility start time. Omitted or null stores publish_at as null, meaning the announcement becomes visible immediately after publish.

name.description: Runtime network name attached to the container.

network_id.description: Runtime network identifier, such as the Docker network ID.

endpoint_id.description: Runtime endpoint identifier for this container attachment within the network.

ip_address.description: Container IP address assigned on this network.

gateway.description: Gateway address reported for this container network attachment.

mac_address.description: MAC address assigned to the container endpoint on this network.

available.description: Compatibility mirror of stats_available for existing clients. New UI code should use stats_available.

unavailable_reason.description: Compatibility mirror of stats_error_key for existing clients.

stats_available.description: True when Docker runtime CPU or memory stats were collected for this row.

stats_error_key.description: Stable sanitized reason stats are absent, such as stats_timeout, stats_unavailable, or stats_incomplete.

stats_error_message.description: Sanitized display-safe stats collection message; raw Docker daemon errors are not exposed.

short_id.description: Runtime id prefix suitable for list display.

name.description: Primary display name, falling back to id when the runtime returns no names.

health.description: Nullable when the runtime cannot determine health on the list path without row-level inspect.

primary_ip.description: Primary IP address when the runtime list summary exposes one without raw inspect.

networks.description: Low-cost network attachment summary from the runtime list path.

restart_count.description: Nullable when the runtime list path does not expose restart count without inspect.

compose_project.description: Docker Compose project label when present.

compose_service.description: Docker Compose service label when present.

endpoint.description: Runtime endpoint label. Sensitive credentials must not be included.

force.description: Applies only to remove. Force remove running containers when true.

container-batch-action-response.description: Batch action result summary. The items array contains exactly one result item for each requested container id and preserves the request id order so callers can correlate each result by position as well as by id.

items.description: Per-container action results in the same order as the requested container ids, with one response item per requested id. OpenAPI cannot express equality with the request array length, so clients should rely on this contract text plus each item's id for correlation.

value.description: Environment variable value. Omitted when the active policy hides or masks the value.

masked.description: Whether the value is intentionally omitted by environment display policy.

sensitive.description: Whether the key matched the container module sensitive-key heuristic.

source.description: Runtime source of the environment variable entry.

container-environment-entry.description: Container environment variable entry after policy application.

type.description: Runtime mount type such as bind, volume, or tmpfs.

environment_policy.description: Effective container environment variable display policy applied to this detail response.

container-detail.description: Container detail returns environment variables according to the configured display policy and omits raw inspect payload fields that may contain secrets.

since.description: Echoes the accepted since query when provided.

truncated.description: True when runtime output exceeded the bounded tail window.

message.description: Existing runtime fallback text paired with messageKey for compatibility.

messageKey.description: Stable error localization key. Consumers should prefer this key and use message only as fallback text.

locale.description: Locale used to resolve the fallback message text in this response.

data.description: Optional structured error details preserved from the current runtime.

force.description: Force remove a running container. Defaults to false and must be explicitly enabled by the caller.

action_label_key.description: Optional key-first action label for this alert row.

action_label.description: Optional fallback action label for this alert row.

healthy_modules.description: Optional count of healthy modules for module-runtime health summaries.

abnormal_services.description: Optional count of module or service entries that need attention.

bearerAuth.description: Existing access-token authentication via the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.

refreshCookie.name: graft_refresh_token
refreshCookie.description:
Documents the refresh cookie transport used by auth refresh/logout flows. The runtime may
override the cookie name from auth config; this spec keeps the current default name as the
documented baseline.

locale-header.name: X-Graft-Locale
locale-header.description: Explicit locale override header already supported by the runtime.

request-id-header.name: X-Request-Id
request-id-header.description:
Optional caller-supplied request id. If omitted, the runtime generates one and echoes it
through the response header and envelope traceId field.

session-list-limit.name: limit
session-list-limit.description: Optional maximum number of sessions to return. The runtime accepts values from 1 to 100.

fullWindow.summary: Fetch up to the maximum supported window

trend-range-query.name: trend_range
trend-range-query.description: Optional trend window. Invalid values currently fall back to the backend default `10m`.

scheduled-task-list-limit.name: limit
scheduled-task-list-limit.description: Optional maximum number of scheduled tasks to return. The runtime accepts values from 1 to 100.

firstTwenty.summary: Fetch the default scheduled task window

fullWindow.summary: Fetch up to the maximum supported window

scheduled-task-list-offset.name: offset
scheduled-task-list-offset.description: Optional zero-based offset for scheduled tasks.

firstPage.summary: Start from the first scheduled task

scheduled-task-run-list-limit.name: limit
scheduled-task-run-list-limit.description: Optional maximum number of scheduled task runs to return. The runtime accepts values from 1 to 100.

recentTwenty.summary: Fetch the default recent run window

fullWindow.summary: Fetch up to the maximum supported window

scheduled-task-run-list-offset.name: offset
scheduled-task-run-list-offset.description: Optional zero-based offset for scheduled task run history.

container-list-limit.name: limit
container-list-limit.description: Optional maximum number of containers to return. The runtime accepts values from 1 to 100.

defaultWindow.summary: Fetch the default container list window

maxWindow.summary: Fetch up to the maximum supported window

container-list-keyword.name: keyword
container-list-keyword.description: Optional case-insensitive keyword matched against id, short id, name, image, status, runtime, ports, labels, compose metadata, and low-cost network fields.

container-list-health.name: health
container-list-health.description: Optional health filter. Containers whose list row cannot cheaply determine health are excluded when a specific health filter is provided.

container-id-path.name: id
container-id-path.description: Container id or name. Clients must call encodeURIComponent before placing this value in the path. The backend must PathUnescape the path parameter and reject empty values, slashes, and control characters with ops.container.error.invalidContainerRef.

container-logs-tail.name: tail
container-logs-tail.description: Number of log lines to return from the end of the stream.

container-logs-since.name: since
container-logs-since.description: Optional log lower bound. Accepts an RFC3339 timestamp or a duration such as 10m, 1h, or 24h. Invalid values must return a localized validation error.

container-logs-timestamps.name: timestamps
container-logs-timestamps.description: Whether each returned log line should include the runtime-provided timestamp.

container-logs-stdout.name: stdout
container-logs-stdout.description: Whether stdout stream lines should be included.

container-logs-stderr.name: stderr
container-logs-stderr.description: Whether stderr stream lines should be included.

request-id.description: Stable request identifier echoed by the runtime.

unauthorized.description: Unauthorized request under existing error envelope semantics.

internal-server-error.description: Internal server error under existing error envelope semantics.

forbidden.description: Forbidden request under existing error envelope semantics.
